channel.title: Blog on Andrew Black
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Andrew Black

item.title: Australian Data Science Network Conference
item.description: I am on the organising committee for the upcoming Australian Data Science Network 2023 Conference, which will be held in Adelaide, 7-8th of December.

item.title: What programming language should I learn first (as a maths student)?
item.description:
This post summarises my answer to a common question I get from mathematics students.

I’m not dogmatic about this; I recommend either R, Julia or Python depending on your project and specialisation. If you’re doing statistics, R is a no-brainer. For machine-learning heavy work, Python is still the standard. For more general numerical computing and simulation, for example Monte Carlo and MCMC (most of what I research day to day) I recommend Julia now because these things need to be fast. In the past I would have recommended C/C++ for simulations but I now believe Julia is more than good enough and much simpler to get going with.

item.title: Applied Probability at the Rock
item.description: I created the poster for the upcoming Applied Probability conference being held for Phil Pollett’s 60’th birthday. Inspired by some of Anders Hoff’s work, I tried to capture the layer structure of Uluru using a line tracing algorithm written in Processing, which uses Java behind the scenes. Roughly, it starts with a straight line and then tries to copy this, but with some noise added. After many iterations larger scale patterns start to emerge.
